source: Android BDDeviceSessionImpl/BleDeviceSession tests, iOS CBDeviceSessionImpl/CBDeviceListener/CBScanner tests, and shared fake transport planning audit
description: Pins the host-owned decision for BLE/session state machines because current transitions are coupled to Android BluetoothGatt or iOS CoreBluetooth host behavior.

expected.commonDecision: Do not introduce shared BLE/session state-machine planning until a future slice defines a deterministic contract independent of Android BluetoothGatt, Bluedroid callbacks, operation queues, advertisement timestamps, iOS CoreBluetooth manager state, scanner queues, permissions, lifecycle, and public error mapping.
androidEvidence: BDDeviceSessionImplTest resetGatt closes and clears BluetoothGatt
BDDeviceSessionImplTest authentication checks inspect BluetoothGatt services and platform clients
BleDeviceSessionTest aliveness depends on session state and advertisement timestamp windows
iosEvidence: CBScannerTest scanning transitions depend on CBCentralManager powered state and queued scanner actions
CBDeviceListenerImplTest observers depend on Combine/lifetime behavior
CBDeviceSessionImplTest pairing classification depends on CoreBluetooth error domains

notes: This is a guarded host-owned vector. It does not authorize shared runtime delegation; it documents why the current state transitions remain host-owned.
